title: Dense Smoky Comforting Thai Meat Larb
description: A rich, bold, comforting Thai meat larb dish featuring the smoky flavors of red wine and the tender crumb of double-rise bread, with a focus on plant-based ingredients. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or a cozy evening in.
instructions: In a large pan over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon of oil and cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the red wine to the pan and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, stirring occasionally.
Add the soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, ginger, garlic, coconut sugar, and rice vinegar to the pan and stir to combine.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the double-rise bread to the pan and cook until it is well coated with the meat mixture and is starting to crumble.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving the larb hot.
Serve the larb hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
This dish is best served immediately, but it can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months if desired.
To reheat the larb, cook it in a pan over medium heat until it is hot and crispy.
Adjust the seasoning of the larb as needed before serving.
Consider serving the larb with steamed vegetables, a simple salad, or additional double-rise bread for a satisfying meal.
For an extra rich and indulgent touch, consider topping the larb with a fried egg or a sprinkle of chopped fresh herbs like cilantro or basil.
If desired, serve the larb with a side of steamed jasmine rice for a more traditional Thai meal.
To make the larb more substantial, consider adding cooked vegetables like bell peppers or zucchini to the pan with the meat mixture.
To make the larb more spicy, consider adding diced jalapenos or serrano peppers to the pan with the meat mixture.
To make the larb more substantial, consider adding cooked noodles like rice noodles or egg noodles to the pan with the meat mixture.
To make the larb more flavorful, consider adding a few sprigs of fresh mint or basil to the pan with the meat mixture.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
